evidence.snippet:
### [FLYC-300 Series](/en/product/processor/gpu-computing/flyc-300)
#### Low-SWaP AI Mission Computer Powered by NVIDIA® Jetson Orin™ NX
#### SWaP-Optimized AI Mission Computer Enabling Unmanned System Applications

items.notes: FLYC-300 is a Low-SWaP AI Mission Computer for drones/unmanned systems, powering autonomous UAV missions. NRU Jetson-based series also targets drone/AMR applications.
NVIDIA Jetson-based NRU series products support GMSL camera integration and AI sensor fusion for drone/AMR navigation tasks.

products_categories.notes: Neousys is primarily a broad industrial rugged computing company; drone-specific products are a focused vertical around AI mission computers.

headquarters.notes: Careers link goes to 104.com.tw (Taiwan's leading job portal). Site also uses Traditional Chinese (繁體中文) and has a separate China subsidiary (neousys.cn). Worldwide-office page was skipped; exact city/address not confirmed from corpus.

evidence.snippet:
### [FLYC-300 Series](/en/product/processor/gpu-computing/flyc-300)
#### Low-SWaP AI Mission Computer Powered by NVIDIA® Jetson Orin™ NX
#### SWaP-Optimized AI Mission Computer Enabling Unmanned System Applications

From mission computers to compact edge systems, Neousys delivers scalable AI computing for defense and simulation, enabling reliable deployment, real-time processing, and flexible integration in extreme environments.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Neousys makes drone-specific mission computers (FLYC-300) and rugged NVIDIA Jetson-based compute subsystems marketed for UAV/unmanned system integration. Primary business is broader industrial computing, but drone subsystems are an explicit product line.

items.name: SEMIL series
items.notes: MIL-STD ruggedized compute platforms for mission-critical defense applications. Multiple variants: SEMIL-2000, SEMIL-2200, SEMIL-2000GC, SEMIL-2200GC.

items.name: Nuvo series
items.product_type: Rugged fanless industrial PCs / Edge AI GPU computers

items.notes: Large family of Intel/AMD rugged fanless computers; includes GPU variants (GC suffix). Multiple generations from Nuvo-7000 to Nuvo-11000.

products.notes: Full product catalog is very large; only drone-relevant and key product families listed. FLYC-300 product page was skipped in the crawl.

ndaa.notes: No NDAA Section 848/889 claims found anywhere in the fetched corpus. Neousys is a Taiwanese manufacturer; NDAA compliance is not mentioned on their website.

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS / Blue sUAS claims found in the corpus.

readiness.notes: Full production catalog available (2026 Catalog Version A1), ISO 9001 certified, 'Get Quote' ordering capability. Products have defined SKUs and are listed with specifications for immediate procurement.

unresolved_questions: Headquarters exact city/address not in fetched corpus (worldwide-office page skipped)

fetch_requests.reason: Would confirm Neousys headquarters city, country, and street address - currently marked as Taiwan based on indirect evidence only.
FLYC-300 drone mission computer product page - would provide detailed specs, applications, and use cases confirming drone supply chain role and flight_and_vehicle_control category.
About page would confirm headquarters location, founding year, and company background, potentially also mentioning NDAA or compliance status.

tagline: Manufactures rugged fanless industrial computers (Nuvo, SEMIL, POC series) and edge AI platforms with GPU support, plus NVIDIA Jetson-based vision systems (NRU series with GMSL2 camera hubs). Primary markets are vehicle/railway embedded computing and harsh-environment applications. No public evidence of NDAA compliance, Blue UAS certification, or named defense contracts; gaps exist regarding unmanned-systems integration maturity and U.S. supply-chain qualification status.
